title: Evidence Foundry Rule-Provenance Sidecar
description: Validates modules/<moduleId>/rule-provenance.json (evidence-foundry-buildout P3-T6, FR-15, `02 §4.13`). `schemas/rule.schema.json` permits exactly the 14 governance-hardened fields it already requires (`id`/`category`/`when`/`evidence`/`output` plus the EP-4 governance fields) and rejects everything else via `additionalProperties: false` — so a drafted rule's rich authoring metadata (the decision it was drafted from, the rf claim/assertion ids it traces to, missingness handling, local-profile requirements, planned test-case ids, review status, and any field the strict schema has no slot for at all, e.g. `supersedes`) is never silently discarded; it lives here instead, joined back to the strict rule record by `ruleId`. Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, or that a named human clinician reviewed anything — `reviewStatus` legally stays `draft` through E0. scripts/validate-kb.mjs wires this schema in as an EXISTENCE-GATED check: not every module carries this file yet (Phase 3 stages it only under build/kb-pack/; Phase 4 migrates it into modules/cbc_suite_v1/), so its absence is not itself an error.

rfProvenance.description: The single upstream rf run/bundle every entry in this file traces back to (mirrors modules/<id>/evidence-assertions.json's and modules/<id>/authoring-decisions.yaml's own `rfProvenance` blocks, P3-T3/P3-T1) — kept once at the document level rather than repeated per entry.

entries.description: `minItems: 0` (mirrors schemas/evidence-assertions.schema.json's and schemas/authoring-decisions.schema.json's own P3-T3/P3-T4 relaxation): a brand-new module package scaffold legitimately has zero provenance entries until a rule is drafted.

entry.description: One rule's provenance sidecar record (`02 §4.13`'s worked-example shape, extended with the `reviewBy`/`supersedes` fields the strict `rules.json` projection also has no slot for). Every numeric threshold or age boundary the joined strict rule contains must trace, via `basis.evidenceAssertionIds[]`, to a real modules/<id>/evidence-assertions.json record — this schema cannot itself verify that cross-file resolution (a traceability-index concern, P5-T4), but requires the field to be present and non-empty here.

ruleId.description: Join key back to the strict runtime `rules.json` record's own `id` (same pattern as schemas/rule.schema.json's `id` field) — proven by a cross-file test, not merely schema-shape-legal in isolation.

moduleId.description: Must equal the enclosing document's `moduleId` — this schema validates the entry in isolation and cannot cross-check the parent; that equality is enforced by scripts/validate-kb.mjs's validateRuleProvenance().

basis.description: `02 §4.13`'s companion-provenance `basis` object. `decisionId` is REQUIRED (R-P2 analog, this task's binding acceptance criteria: 'a rule-provenance.json entry missing basis.decisionId fails schema validation') — a drafted rule with no named authoring decision has no reviewable basis for its Boolean logic (FR-14) at all.

kind.description: Exactly the 4 `basis.kind` values `02 §4.11`'s routing table names (mirrors schemas/authoring-decisions.schema.json's own `basis.kind` enum).

decisionId.description: Must resolve to a real `decision_id` in modules/<id>/authoring-decisions.yaml (P3-T1) — this schema cannot verify that cross-file resolution itself.

rfClaimIds.description: REQUIRED, non-empty. Every entry must resolve to a real `claim_id` in the upstream bundle's claims/claim_ledger.yaml.

evidenceAssertionIds.description: REQUIRED, non-empty. Every entry must resolve to a real `assertionId` in modules/<id>/evidence-assertions.json (P3-T3) — the exact-passage locator that keeps the joined rule from carrying an invented threshold.

missingness.description: Honest, code-grounded statement of what happens when this rule's `when`-clause fact(s) are absent/unknown at runtime (per the module's actual deriveFacts behavior, never invented) — e.g. whether the rule silently fails to match, or a separate missing-data question rule exists. `02 §4.13`'s own worked example: `no_match_and_emit_required-data-question`.

localProfileRequirement.description: The local/analyzer-specific reference-range or lab-context profile this rule's interpretation depends on, or `null` when the rule's logic does not depend on one (e.g. a fixed universal threshold or an age-only scope check). `02 §4.13`'s own worked example: `ferritin_assay_and_inflammation_context`.

testIds.description: Planned/linked engine-test-case ids for this rule (`02 §4.13`'s own worked example lists 4: positive/negative/boundary/missingness). Legitimately empty at this drafting stage — the test corpus is a Phase 4 deliverable (P4-T5..T8), not fabricated here.

reviewStatus.description: REQUIRED, non-null, closed enum. `draft` is the only legal value while this rule is still a staged proposal (E0) — never `approved` absent a real, named clinical review process, which does not exist in this build (CLAUDE.md: 'No AI-published rule changes').

reviewBy.description: Governance housekeeping due-date for the next currency check on this rule's evidence basis (mirrors the authoring `rule-proposals.json` record's own `reviewBy` field, which `schemas/rule.schema.json` has no slot for). `null` legal when not yet set.

supersedes.description: Rule id this rule supersedes/replaces, or `null`. `schemas/rule.schema.json` has no slot for supersession at all (`02 §4.13`'s field-mapping table sends it here unconditionally) — this field exists so that fact is never silently dropped rather than merely deferred.

authoringNotes.description: Optional. Carries forward the drafted rule-proposal's own `authoringNotes` (P3-T5, tools/rf-bundle-to-kb-pack/lib/rule-candidate-drafts.mjs) — known limitations/proxy-fact caveats the strict `rules.json` projection has no slot for and must not silently drop. Not required: a future non-drafted (e.g. directly authored) rule may legitimately have none.
